gpt-4o.notes: The figure captures the BRDF setup with useful controls, but the 3D interpretation, plane shape/view, and several colors/proportions differ noticeably from the textbook image.

discrepancies: Extra interactive UI (sliders, dropdown, buttons) overlays the scene, not present in source figure
More outgoing green arrows are present and less structured than in the original
Text label for l_out differs: ell_out=F(...) is less legible/formulaic than the TeX-style source
Incoming light arrow coloring is somewhat less gradient and less sharp than original
Right angle marker and circle at the base are thicker/more emphasized than in the 2D source
gpt-4.1.notes: The main geometry is well-captured (surface, normal, incoming/outgoing rays), but interactions and arrows diverge from the original's minimal, textbook composition.
action_items: Reduce the number of outgoing green arrows and make their directions and lengths more closely match the specific ones in the reference image
Refine labels to match original LaTeX styling/positioning, especially for \ell_{out} and other math symbols
Improve the incoming ray gradient to be more like the smooth yellow-to-red transition in the original

discrepancies: The label for outgoing light is truncated to 'ℓout=F(...)' instead of the full formula 'ℓout = F(ℓin, n, λ, p, q)'
The green scatter arrows (spray) are randomly generated rather than matching the specific, clean set of symmetric arrows in the textbook figure
The right-angle indicator is slightly misaligned with the 3D plane of the normal and the coordinate axes
gemini-3.5-flash.notes: Excellent interactive demonstration of BRDF concepts with multiple presets, though one equation label is simplified/truncated.
action_items: Expand the 'lout' label to display the full equation: ℓ_out = F(ℓ_in, n, λ, p, q)
Hardcode the green spray directions and lengths for the 'Textbook View' to match the neat, symmetric fan of rays in the original diagram
Align the right-angle indicator dynamically to face the camera or align perfectly with the sagittal plane of the vectors
